<!-- Generated by scripts/generate_cli_reference.py. Do not edit manually. -->

# CLI Reference

This page is generated from pytask's Click command tree.

Running `pytask` without a subcommand is equivalent to `pytask build`.

## Command Index

- [`pytask build`](#pytask-build): Collect tasks, execute them and report the results.
- [`pytask clean`](#pytask-clean): Clean the provided paths by removing files unknown to
    pytask.
- [`pytask collect`](#pytask-collect): Collect tasks and report information about them.
- [`pytask dag`](#pytask-dag): Create a visualization of the directed acyclic graph.
- [`pytask markers`](#pytask-markers): Show all registered markers.
- [`pytask profile`](#pytask-profile): Show information about resource consumption.

## pytask { #pytask }

Manage your tasks with pytask.

**Usage**

```console
pytask [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...
```

**Commands**

- [`pytask build`](#pytask-build): Collect tasks, execute them and report the results.
- [`pytask clean`](#pytask-clean): Clean the provided paths by removing files unknown to
    pytask.
- [`pytask collect`](#pytask-collect): Collect tasks and report information about them.
- [`pytask dag`](#pytask-dag): Create a visualization of the directed acyclic graph.
- [`pytask markers`](#pytask-markers): Show all registered markers.
- [`pytask profile`](#pytask-profile): Show information about resource consumption.

**Options**

<dl class="cli-reference">
<dt id="pytask--version"><a href="#pytask--version"><code>--version</code></a></dt><dd><p>Show the version and exit.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask--help"><a href="#pytask--help"><code>--help</code></a></dt><dd><p>Show this message and exit.</p></dd>
</dl>

## pytask build { #pytask-build }

Collect tasks, execute them and report the results.

**Usage**

```console
pytask build [OPTIONS] [PATHS]...
```

**Arguments**

<dl class="cli-reference">
<dt id="pytask-build--paths"><a href="#pytask-build--paths"><code>[PATHS]...</code></a></dt><dd><p>Paths where pytask looks for task files and configuration.</p></dd>
</dl>

**Options**

<dl class="cli-reference">
<dt id="pytask-build--config"><a href="#pytask-build--config"><code>-c, --config FILE</code></a></dt><dd><p>Path to configuration file.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--capture"><a href="#pytask-build--capture"><code>--capture [fd|no|sys|tee-sys]</code></a></dt><dd><p>Per task capturing method.</p><p>Default: fd<br>Accepted values: fd, no, sys, tee-sys</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--clean-lockfile"><a href="#pytask-build--clean-lockfile"><code>--clean-lockfile</code></a></dt><dd><p>Rewrite the lockfile with only currently collected tasks.</p><p>Default: false</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--database-url"><a href="#pytask-build--database-url"><code>--database-url TEXT</code></a></dt><dd><p>Url to the database.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--debug-pytask"><a href="#pytask-build--debug-pytask"><code>--debug-pytask</code></a></dt><dd><p>Trace all function calls in the plugin framework.</p><p>Default: false</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--disable-warnings"><a href="#pytask-build--disable-warnings"><code>--disable-warnings</code></a></dt><dd><p>Disables the summary for warnings.</p><p>Default: false</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--dry-run"><a href="#pytask-build--dry-run"><code>--dry-run</code></a></dt><dd><p>Perform a dry-run.</p><p>Default: false</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--editor-url-scheme"><a href="#pytask-build--editor-url-scheme"><code>--editor-url-scheme TEXT</code></a></dt><dd><p>Use file, vscode, pycharm or a custom url scheme to add URLs to task ids to quickly jump to the task definition. Use no_link to disable URLs.</p><p>Default: file</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--explain"><a href="#pytask-build--explain"><code>--explain</code></a></dt><dd><p>Explain why tasks need to be executed by showing what changed.</p><p>Default: false</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--force"><a href="#pytask-build--force"><code>-f, --force</code></a></dt><dd><p>Execute a task even if it succeeded successfully before.</p><p>Default: false</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--hook-module"><a href="#pytask-build--hook-module"><code>--hook-module TEXT</code></a></dt><dd><p>Path to a Python module that contains hook implementations.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--ignore"><a href="#pytask-build--ignore"><code>--ignore TEXT</code></a></dt><dd><p>A pattern to ignore files or directories. Refer to &#x27;pathlib.Path.match&#x27; for more info.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--k"><a href="#pytask-build--k"><code>-k EXPRESSION</code></a></dt><dd><p>Select tasks via expressions on task ids.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--log-cli"><a href="#pytask-build--log-cli"><code>--log-cli / --no-log-cli</code></a></dt><dd><p>Enable live log display during task execution.</p><p>Default: --no-log-cli</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--log-cli-date-format"><a href="#pytask-build--log-cli-date-format"><code>--log-cli-date-format TEXT</code></a></dt><dd><p>Log date format used by the logging module for live logs.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--log-cli-format"><a href="#pytask-build--log-cli-format"><code>--log-cli-format TEXT</code></a></dt><dd><p>Log format used by the logging module for live logs.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--log-cli-level"><a href="#pytask-build--log-cli-level"><code>--log-cli-level LEVEL</code></a></dt><dd><p>CLI logging level.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--log-date-format"><a href="#pytask-build--log-date-format"><code>--log-date-format TEXT</code></a></dt><dd><p>Log date format used by the logging module.</p><p>Default: %H:%M:%S</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--log-file"><a href="#pytask-build--log-file"><code>--log-file TEXT</code></a></dt><dd><p>Path to a file where logging will be written.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--log-file-date-format"><a href="#pytask-build--log-file-date-format"><code>--log-file-date-format TEXT</code></a></dt><dd><p>Log date format used by the logging module for the log file.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--log-file-format"><a href="#pytask-build--log-file-format"><code>--log-file-format TEXT</code></a></dt><dd><p>Log format used by the logging module for the log file.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--log-file-level"><a href="#pytask-build--log-file-level"><code>--log-file-level LEVEL</code></a></dt><dd><p>Log file logging level.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--log-file-mode"><a href="#pytask-build--log-file-mode"><code>--log-file-mode [w|a]</code></a></dt><dd><p>Log file open mode.</p><p>Default: w<br>Accepted values: w, a</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--log-format"><a href="#pytask-build--log-format"><code>--log-format TEXT</code></a></dt><dd><p>Log format used by the logging module.</p><p>Default: %(levelname)-8s %(name)s:%(filename)s:%(lineno)d %(message)s</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--log-level"><a href="#pytask-build--log-level"><code>--log-level LEVEL</code></a></dt><dd><p>Level of messages to catch/display. Not set by default, so it depends on the logger configuration.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--m"><a href="#pytask-build--m"><code>-m MARKER_EXPRESSION</code></a></dt><dd><p>Select tasks via marker expressions.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--max-failures"><a href="#pytask-build--max-failures"><code>--max-failures FLOAT RANGE</code></a></dt><dd><p>Stop after some failures.</p><p>Default: inf</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--n-entries-in-table"><a href="#pytask-build--n-entries-in-table"><code>--n-entries-in-table INTEGER RANGE</code></a></dt><dd><p>How many entries to display in the table during the execution. Tasks which are running are always displayed.</p><p>Default: 15</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--pdb"><a href="#pytask-build--pdb"><code>--pdb</code></a></dt><dd><p>Start the interactive debugger on errors.</p><p>Default: false</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--pdbcls"><a href="#pytask-build--pdbcls"><code>--pdbcls module_name:class_name</code></a></dt><dd><p>Start a custom debugger on errors. For example: --pdbcls=IPython.terminal.debugger:TerminalPdb</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--s"><a href="#pytask-build--s"><code>-s</code></a></dt><dd><p>Shortcut for --capture=no.</p><p>Default: false</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--show-capture"><a href="#pytask-build--show-capture"><code>--show-capture [no|stdout|stderr|log|all]</code></a></dt><dd><p>Choose which captured output should be shown for failed tasks.</p><p>Default: all<br>Accepted values: no, stdout, stderr, log, all</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--show-errors-immediately"><a href="#pytask-build--show-errors-immediately"><code>--show-errors-immediately</code></a></dt><dd><p>Show errors with tracebacks as soon as the task fails.</p><p>Default: false</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--show-locals"><a href="#pytask-build--show-locals"><code>--show-locals</code></a></dt><dd><p>Show local variables in tracebacks.</p><p>Default: false</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--show-traceback"><a href="#pytask-build--show-traceback"><code>--show-traceback / --show-no-traceback</code></a></dt><dd><p>Choose whether tracebacks should be displayed or not.</p><p>Default: --show-traceback</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--sort-table"><a href="#pytask-build--sort-table"><code>--sort-table / --do-not-sort-table</code></a></dt><dd><p>Sort the table of tasks at the end of the execution.</p><p>Default: --sort-table</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--strict-markers"><a href="#pytask-build--strict-markers"><code>--strict-markers</code></a></dt><dd><p>Raise errors for unknown markers.</p><p>Default: false</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--trace"><a href="#pytask-build--trace"><code>--trace</code></a></dt><dd><p>Enter debugger in the beginning of each task.</p><p>Default: false</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--verbose"><a href="#pytask-build--verbose"><code>-v, --verbose INTEGER RANGE</code></a></dt><dd><p>Make pytask verbose (&gt;= 0) or quiet (= 0).</p><p>Default: 1</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--stop-after-first-failure"><a href="#pytask-build--stop-after-first-failure"><code>-x, --stop-after-first-failure</code></a></dt><dd><p>Stop after the first failure.</p><p>Default: false</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-build--help"><a href="#pytask-build--help"><code>--help</code></a></dt><dd><p>Show this message and exit.</p></dd>
</dl>

## pytask clean { #pytask-clean }

Clean the provided paths by removing files unknown to pytask.

**Usage**

```console
pytask clean [OPTIONS] [PATHS]...
```

**Arguments**

<dl class="cli-reference">
<dt id="pytask-clean--paths"><a href="#pytask-clean--paths"><code>[PATHS]...</code></a></dt><dd><p>Paths where pytask looks for task files and configuration.</p></dd>
</dl>

**Options**

<dl class="cli-reference">
<dt id="pytask-clean--config"><a href="#pytask-clean--config"><code>-c, --config FILE</code></a></dt><dd><p>Path to configuration file.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-clean--directories"><a href="#pytask-clean--directories"><code>-d, --directories</code></a></dt><dd><p>Remove whole directories.</p><p>Default: false</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-clean--database-url"><a href="#pytask-clean--database-url"><code>--database-url TEXT</code></a></dt><dd><p>Url to the database.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-clean--exclude"><a href="#pytask-clean--exclude"><code>-e, --exclude PATTERN</code></a></dt><dd><p>A filename pattern to exclude files from the cleaning process.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-clean--editor-url-scheme"><a href="#pytask-clean--editor-url-scheme"><code>--editor-url-scheme TEXT</code></a></dt><dd><p>Use file, vscode, pycharm or a custom url scheme to add URLs to task ids to quickly jump to the task definition. Use no_link to disable URLs.</p><p>Default: file</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-clean--hook-module"><a href="#pytask-clean--hook-module"><code>--hook-module TEXT</code></a></dt><dd><p>Path to a Python module that contains hook implementations.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-clean--ignore"><a href="#pytask-clean--ignore"><code>--ignore TEXT</code></a></dt><dd><p>A pattern to ignore files or directories. Refer to &#x27;pathlib.Path.match&#x27; for more info.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-clean--k"><a href="#pytask-clean--k"><code>-k EXPRESSION</code></a></dt><dd><p>Select tasks via expressions on task ids.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-clean--m"><a href="#pytask-clean--m"><code>-m MARKER_EXPRESSION</code></a></dt><dd><p>Select tasks via marker expressions.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-clean--mode"><a href="#pytask-clean--mode"><code>--mode [dry-run|force|interactive]</code></a></dt><dd><p>Choose &#x27;dry-run&#x27; to print the paths of files/directories which would be removed, &#x27;interactive&#x27; for a confirmation prompt for every path, and &#x27;force&#x27; to remove all unknown paths at once.</p><p>Default: dry-run<br>Accepted values: dry-run, force, interactive</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-clean--quiet"><a href="#pytask-clean--quiet"><code>-q, --quiet</code></a></dt><dd><p>Do not print the names of the removed paths.</p><p>Default: false</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-clean--strict-markers"><a href="#pytask-clean--strict-markers"><code>--strict-markers</code></a></dt><dd><p>Raise errors for unknown markers.</p><p>Default: false</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-clean--help"><a href="#pytask-clean--help"><code>--help</code></a></dt><dd><p>Show this message and exit.</p></dd>
</dl>

## pytask collect { #pytask-collect }

Collect tasks and report information about them.

**Usage**

```console
pytask collect [OPTIONS] [PATHS]...
```

**Arguments**

<dl class="cli-reference">
<dt id="pytask-collect--paths"><a href="#pytask-collect--paths"><code>[PATHS]...</code></a></dt><dd><p>Paths where pytask looks for task files and configuration.</p></dd>
</dl>

**Options**

<dl class="cli-reference">
<dt id="pytask-collect--config"><a href="#pytask-collect--config"><code>-c, --config FILE</code></a></dt><dd><p>Path to configuration file.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-collect--database-url"><a href="#pytask-collect--database-url"><code>--database-url TEXT</code></a></dt><dd><p>Url to the database.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-collect--editor-url-scheme"><a href="#pytask-collect--editor-url-scheme"><code>--editor-url-scheme TEXT</code></a></dt><dd><p>Use file, vscode, pycharm or a custom url scheme to add URLs to task ids to quickly jump to the task definition. Use no_link to disable URLs.</p><p>Default: file</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-collect--hook-module"><a href="#pytask-collect--hook-module"><code>--hook-module TEXT</code></a></dt><dd><p>Path to a Python module that contains hook implementations.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-collect--ignore"><a href="#pytask-collect--ignore"><code>--ignore TEXT</code></a></dt><dd><p>A pattern to ignore files or directories. Refer to &#x27;pathlib.Path.match&#x27; for more info.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-collect--k"><a href="#pytask-collect--k"><code>-k EXPRESSION</code></a></dt><dd><p>Select tasks via expressions on task ids.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-collect--m"><a href="#pytask-collect--m"><code>-m MARKER_EXPRESSION</code></a></dt><dd><p>Select tasks via marker expressions.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-collect--nodes"><a href="#pytask-collect--nodes"><code>--nodes</code></a></dt><dd><p>Show a task&#x27;s dependencies and products.</p><p>Default: false</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-collect--strict-markers"><a href="#pytask-collect--strict-markers"><code>--strict-markers</code></a></dt><dd><p>Raise errors for unknown markers.</p><p>Default: false</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-collect--help"><a href="#pytask-collect--help"><code>--help</code></a></dt><dd><p>Show this message and exit.</p></dd>
</dl>

## pytask dag { #pytask-dag }

Create a visualization of the directed acyclic graph.

**Usage**

```console
pytask dag [OPTIONS] [PATHS]...
```

**Arguments**

<dl class="cli-reference">
<dt id="pytask-dag--paths"><a href="#pytask-dag--paths"><code>[PATHS]...</code></a></dt><dd><p>Paths where pytask looks for task files and configuration.</p></dd>
</dl>

**Options**

<dl class="cli-reference">
<dt id="pytask-dag--config"><a href="#pytask-dag--config"><code>-c, --config FILE</code></a></dt><dd><p>Path to configuration file.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-dag--database-url"><a href="#pytask-dag--database-url"><code>--database-url TEXT</code></a></dt><dd><p>Url to the database.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-dag--hook-module"><a href="#pytask-dag--hook-module"><code>--hook-module TEXT</code></a></dt><dd><p>Path to a Python module that contains hook implementations.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-dag--layout"><a href="#pytask-dag--layout"><code>-l, --layout TEXT</code></a></dt><dd><p>The layout determines the structure of the graph. Here you find an overview of all available layouts: https://graphviz.org/docs/layouts.</p><p>Default: dot</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-dag--output-path"><a href="#pytask-dag--output-path"><code>-o, --output-path FILE</code></a></dt><dd><p>The output path of the visualization. The format is inferred from the file extension.</p><p>Default: dag.pdf</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-dag--rank-direction"><a href="#pytask-dag--rank-direction"><code>-r, --rank-direction [TB|LR|BT|RL]</code></a></dt><dd><p>The direction of the directed graph. It can be ordered from top to bottom, TB, left to right, LR, bottom to top, BT, or right to left, RL.</p><p>Default: TB<br>Accepted values: TB, LR, BT, RL</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-dag--help"><a href="#pytask-dag--help"><code>--help</code></a></dt><dd><p>Show this message and exit.</p></dd>
</dl>

## pytask profile { #pytask-profile }

Show information about resource consumption.

**Usage**

```console
pytask profile [OPTIONS] [PATHS]...
```

**Arguments**

<dl class="cli-reference">
<dt id="pytask-profile--paths"><a href="#pytask-profile--paths"><code>[PATHS]...</code></a></dt><dd><p>Paths where pytask looks for task files and configuration.</p></dd>
</dl>

**Options**

<dl class="cli-reference">
<dt id="pytask-profile--config"><a href="#pytask-profile--config"><code>-c, --config FILE</code></a></dt><dd><p>Path to configuration file.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-profile--database-url"><a href="#pytask-profile--database-url"><code>--database-url TEXT</code></a></dt><dd><p>Url to the database.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-profile--editor-url-scheme"><a href="#pytask-profile--editor-url-scheme"><code>--editor-url-scheme TEXT</code></a></dt><dd><p>Use file, vscode, pycharm or a custom url scheme to add URLs to task ids to quickly jump to the task definition. Use no_link to disable URLs.</p><p>Default: file</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-profile--export"><a href="#pytask-profile--export"><code>--export [no|json|csv]</code></a></dt><dd><p>Export the profile in the specified format.</p><p>Default: no<br>Accepted values: no, json, csv</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-profile--hook-module"><a href="#pytask-profile--hook-module"><code>--hook-module TEXT</code></a></dt><dd><p>Path to a Python module that contains hook implementations.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-profile--ignore"><a href="#pytask-profile--ignore"><code>--ignore TEXT</code></a></dt><dd><p>A pattern to ignore files or directories. Refer to &#x27;pathlib.Path.match&#x27; for more info.</p></dd>
<dt id="pytask-profile--help"><a href="#pytask-profile--help"><code>--help</code></a></dt><dd><p>Show this message and exit.</p></dd>
</dl>
